abstract: This paper reviews physicochemical parameters and experimental considerations critical to evaluating the regulatory adequacy of microplastics and nanoplastics (MNPs) (eco)toxicity studies. The authors adapted existing evaluation frameworks (SciRAP and CRED) to address MNP-specific challenges, identifying key assessment criteria including polymer identity, size and shape distribution, surface chemistry, additive content, dispersion behaviour, and test material characterization. The resulting frameworks, SciRAPplastic and plasticCRED, are designed to strengthen reporting practices and support structured evaluation of study reliability and relevance for environmental and health risk assessments of MNPs.
